title.en: Host-based intrusion detection and response solution

group-parts.group: Host-based intrusion detection and response solution
group-parts.name: overview

prose.en: Many security products rely on signatures to detect malicious code. This approach is only effective when malicious code has already been profiled, and signatures are available from security vendors. Unfortunately, malicious actors can easily create variants of known malicious code to bypass traditional signature-based detection. A Host-based Intrusion Prevention System (HIPS) or Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solution can use behaviour-based detection to assist in identifying and blocking anomalous behaviour as well as detecting malicious code that has yet to be identified by security vendors. As such, it is important that either a HIPS or EDR solution is implemented on workstations, critical servers and high-value servers.
